title: Python 3.12 not compile
author: animecomico
body:
I try build for python 3.12, but i have this error:
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
gmake[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/pydegensac.dir/build.make:101: /home/carlosf/pydegensac/build/lib.linux-x86_64-cpython-312/pydegensac/pydegensac.cpython-312-x86_64-linux-gnu.so] Error 1

    setup(
  File "/home/carlosf/anaconda/envs/flowdrone_v2/lib/python3.12/site-packages/setuptools/__init__.py", line 115, in setup

           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/home/carlosf/anaconda/envs/flowdrone_v2/lib/python3.12/site-packages/setuptools/_distutils/core.py", line 186, in setup

    dist.run_commands()
  File "/home/carlosf/anaconda/envs/flowdrone_v2/lib/python3.12/site-packages/setuptools/_distutils/dist.py", line 1002, in run_commands

  File "/home/carlosf/anaconda/envs/flowdrone_v2/lib/python3.12/site-packages/setuptools/_distutils/command/build.py", line 135, in run
    self.run_command(cmd_name)

subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['cmake', '--build', '.', '--config', 'Release', '--', '-j2']' returned non-zero exit status 2.


Please help me.

title: Package does not build unless C++11 standard defined
author: tworkool
body:
I could not build the package, because I got many errors while running the setup. Once I added the following setup it worked:

In CmakeLists:
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD 11)
set(CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED ON)

In setup.py:
add '-std=c++11' to extra_compile_args

title: pydegensac.findHomography() Modify
author: DoongLi
body: Hi, I want to modify the source code of pydegensac.findhomography (), such as modifying Ransac, so that the four pairs are distributed evenly. What should I do?

body:
```

conda 22.9.0

pip 22.3.1 from /home/mona/anaconda3/envs/neuralsfm/lib/python3.10/site-packages/pip (python 3.10)
```

title: pip install doesn't work for any other python version than python3.8
author: yash4242
body:
I was running 3.9.7. Tried to install pydegensac via `pip install pydegensac`, as suggested pyPI and a few other websites. Pip outputs:
```
Defaulting to user installation because normal site-packages is not writeable
ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pydegensac (from versions: none)
ERROR: No matching distribution found for pydegensac
```
Tried to build from source as follows: 
```
git clone https://github.com/ducha-aiki/pydegensac
cd pydegensac-v_0.12/
sudo python3 setup.py build
```
the compilation failed after a some time. The following shows a few usual compile time messages just preceding the error messages and the error message as well:
```
/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/src/pydegensac/degensac/exp_ranF.c:1604:21: warning: ‘error_threshold’ may be used uninitialized in this function [-Wmaybe-uninitialized]

      |                     ^
/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/src/pydegensac/bindings.cpp:295:15: note: ‘FDSidx1’ was declared here
  295 |     FDsidxPtr FDSidx1;
      |               ^
/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/src/pydegensac/degensac/exp_ranF.c:1646:17: warning: ‘EXFDS1’ may be used uninitialized in this function [-Wmaybe-uninitialized]
 1646 |             S = exp_inFranicustom (u, len, inliers, S.I, th, errs, buffer, f, samidxBest, &iterID, inlLimit, *resids + 2*len + (iter_cnt-1)*RESIDS_M*len,EXFDS1,FDS1);
      |                 ^
/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/src/pydegensac/bindings.cpp:294:14: note: ‘EXFDS1’ was declared here
  294 |     exFDsPtr EXFDS1;
      |              ^
/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/src/pydegensac/degensac/exp_ranF.c:1646:17: warning: ‘FDS1’ may be used uninitialized in this function [-Wmaybe-uninitialized]
 1646 |             S = exp_inFranicustom (u, len, inliers, S.I, th, errs, buffer, f, samidxBest, &iterID, inlLimit, *resids + 2*len + (iter_cnt-1)*RESIDS_M*len,EXFDS1,FDS1);
      |                 ^
/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/src/pydegensac/bindings.cpp:293:12: note: ‘FDS1’ was declared here
  293 |     FDsPtr FDS1;
      |            ^
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
gmake[2]: *** [CMakeFiles/pydegensac.dir/build.make:286: ../lib.linux-x86_64-3.9/pydegensac/pydegensac.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so] Error 1
gmake[1]: *** [CMakeFiles/Makefile2:136: CMakeFiles/pydegensac.dir/all] Error 2
gmake: *** [Makefile:103: all] Error 2
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/setup.py", line 100, in <module>
    setup(
  File "/usr/lib/python3/dist-packages/setuptools/__init__.py", line 153, in setup
    return distutils.core.setup(**attrs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/distutils/core.py", line 148, in setup
    dist.run_commands()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/distutils/dist.py", line 966, in run_commands
    self.run_command(cmd)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/distutils/dist.py", line 985, in run_command
    cmd_obj.run()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/distutils/command/build.py", line 135, in run
    self.run_command(cmd_name)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/distutils/cmd.py", line 313, in run_command
    self.distribution.run_command(command)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/distutils/dist.py", line 985, in run_command
    cmd_obj.run()
  File "/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/setup.py", line 38, in run
    self.build_extension(ext)
  File "/home/commander/Desktop/pydegensac-v_0.12/setup.py", line 68, in build_extension
    subprocess.check_call(['cmake', '--build', '.'] + build_args,
  File "/usr/lib/python3.9/subprocess.py", line 373, in check_call
    raise CalledProcessError(retcode, cmd)
subprocess.CalledProcessError: Command '['cmake', '--build', '.', '--config', 'Release', '--', '-j2']' returned non-zero exit status 2.
```
A friend suggested to use specifically python3.8 in a venv, and `pip install pydegensac` worked.
Requesting to make it available to higher python versions also. Or maybe update the `Requirements` in the README to `python==3.8`.

title: About the degeneracy check
author: AoxiangFan
body:
Thank you for the great work!

I am trying to understand the degeneracy check (and updating F) part of the code, but the C code seems to be really hard to decipher. So I want to ask do you have the MATLAB code for the degeneracy part (which appears in the C source code)? Or other instructions on implementing the degeneracy part could also help.

Thanks for your time and looking forward to your reply.

title: How to use it for superglue
author: ChrisLLLLL
body:
Thanks for the excellent work! I want to use it for superglue. But superglue will not get <DMatch>. In other words, i don't know how to get the "tentatives"(draw_matches(kps1, kps2, tentatives, img1, img2, H, mask)). Could you help me answer my doubts?
Looking forward to your reply.

title: Using TPS instead of homography
author: anorak94
body: Is it possible to use thin plate splines instead of the homography matrix estimation in pydegensac. Or for instance any black box deformable shape models.

title: Documentation for parameters in findHomography
author: anorak94
body:
Hey thanks for the wonderful library. I also saw your tutorial for CVPR2020 it was very illuminating. Sorry if my question seems a bit naive. But what is the px_th in pydegensac.findHomography. Is it the error between held out pixels when computing the confidence score after estimating the homography. 

Thanks 
Anurag

title: Windows wheels for pypi/document way to build on windows
author: serhiy-yevtushenko
body:
Hi,

currently the build on windows in completely undocumented.
When trying to install via pip, one gets the following output on windows:
```
ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement pydegensac
ERROR: No matching distribution found for pydegensac
```

When trying to build on windows, one learns, that one needs to install cmake.

But even after installing cmake one learns in addition, that fortran compiler is needed:
```
running build_ext
-- Building for: Visual Studio 16 2019
-- Selecting Windows SDK version 10.0.18362.0 to target Windows 10.0.19042.
-- The C compiler identification is MSVC 19.22.27905.0
-- The CXX compiler identification is MSVC 19.22.27905.0
-- Detecting C compiler ABI info
-- Detecting C compiler ABI info - done
-- Check for working C compiler: C:/Program Files (x86)/Microsoft Visual Studio/2019/BuildTools/VC/Tools/MSVC/14.22.27905/bin/Hostx64/x64/cl.exe - skipped
-- Detecting C compile features
-- Detecting C compile features - done
-- Detecting CXX compiler ABI info
-- Detecting CXX compiler ABI info - done
-- Check for working CXX compiler: C:/Program Files (x86)/Microsoft Visual Studio/2019/BuildTools/VC/Tools/MSVC/14.22.27905/bin/Hostx64/x64/cl.exe - skipped
-- Detecting CXX compile features
-- Detecting CXX compile features - done
-- The Fortran compiler identification is unknown
CMake Error at CMakeLists.txt:6 (enable_language):
  No CMAKE_Fortran_COMPILER could be found.
```

, and it is completely unclear, which one should be setup/used

Having windows wheels/or proper documentation for installation on windows would be of help

title: Mac os wheels for pypi
author: johnwlambert
body:
Thanks for providing this repo and the linux wheels on pypi.

Would you consider adding wheels for macosx to https://pypi.org/project/pydegensac/#files? Ran into some nasty errors when building locally on mac os x
```bash
/Users/johnlambert/Documents/pydegensac/src/pydegensac/matutls/./complex.h:44:16: note: 'catanh' is a builtin
      with type '_Complex double (_Complex double)'
/Users/johnlambert/Documents/pydegensac/src/pydegensac/matutls/cmprt.c:12:28: error: implicitly declaring
      library function 'printf' with type 'int (const char *, ...)' [-Werror,-Wimplicit-function-declaration]

                           ^
/Users/johnlambert/Documents/pydegensac/src/pydegensac/matutls/cmprt.c:12:28: note: include the header
```

title: Multiple instances key point detection
author: Ademord
body:
Hello
I was wondering if I can use this algorithm to detect from one reference image, multiple instances of the object in a second image 

If not, what would you recommend ?

Thanks in advance

title: [feature request] random seed
author: ducha-aiki
body: Accept random seed as input parameter

title: unstable results  while running serveral times
author: fuzzypants123
body:
Hi , I used pydensac  and get prefer results instead of cv2.ransac most of times, thanks for the excellent work! 
But I got a trouble and found that give the same input,  it would produce unstable results, and some of them even worse than cv2.ransac.  Besides,  It will keep the same results for about ten times, but when I try dozens of times, the results will be inconsistent. I understand that the algorithm is somewhat random, but is there any way to reduce this randomness as much as possible?

title: Support PnP/RANSAC for pose estimation?
author: hiyyg
body: Currently, it seems this repo can only solve F, E matrices, is there any plan to support PnP/RANSAC for pose estimation?
